hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 4, Q. Both players called a raise before the cards were dealt.
Turn — 6. Your opponent led out with a bet. You called.
River — Jack. Your opponent fired again — half the pot.

wrongReasons: 10 2 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only one pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
3 6 doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only one pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
8 K doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only one pair wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 3 5

Your opponent flopped a straight and immediately raised to build the pot. They led every remaining street — consistent value betting across all streets is a strong signal of a made hand.
